title: Owner-only delete for rentals on Search Rental results

requirements.summary: Add a per-rental "Delete" button to each SearchRentalResultCard in the /search-rental results grid.
acceptanceCriteria: On /search-rental, every rental card includes a "Delete" button element in the card layout (even if conditionally hidden).
The button’s visible label text is exactly "Delete".

file_operations.description: Add a "Delete" button to the SearchRentalResultCard layout with label text exactly "Delete" (visibility can be conditional per REQ-33). Keep button placement within each rental cell/card. (Authorization component is used app-wide for authentication state; verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.)

requirements.summary: Hide the "Delete" button for non-owners and logged-out users by comparing rental.createdBy to the authenticated principal.
acceptanceCriteria: When logged out, no rental card shows a "Delete" button.
When logged in as User A, only rentals where rental.createdBy == User A’s principal show the "Delete" button.
Rentals created by other users do not show a "Delete" button.

file_operations.description: Use the authorization component’s frontend authentication state (via the existing Internet Identity hook) to get the current user principal and compare it to rental.createdBy; render the "Delete" button only when they match, and hide it entirely otherwise. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

requirements.summary: Implement owner delete workflow with exact confirmation text, call backend delete, and immediately remove the rental from the visible list.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking "Delete" opens a confirmation popup showing exactly: "Are you sure you want to delete this product?".
If the user cancels, no backend call is made and the rental remains visible.
If the user confirms, the app calls the backend rental deletion API for that rental.
After a successful delete, the deleted rental no longer appears in the Search Rental results without requiring a manual page refresh.
Deleting a rental does not create any new records.

file_operations.description: Create a React Query mutation hook that calls the backend capability to delete the selected rental (using the existing actor.deleteRental) and, on success, invalidates relevant React Query caches (e.g., rentals lists and search results). Ensure errors (including authorization traps) are surfaced to the UI caller. (Authorization component is used app-wide for actor auth; verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.)

Wire the "Delete" button to a confirmation popup using the exact message text "Are you sure you want to delete this product?"; only on confirm, trigger the delete mutation for the current rental. On success, invoke a parent callback or local mechanism to remove the deleted rental from the rendered list immediately, and ensure React Query caches are invalidated via the mutation hook. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.

Ensure the deleted rental disappears immediately from the visible results without manual refresh by updating page-level UI state (e.g., maintaining a set of deleted rental identifiers/titles and filtering the displayed rentals) and passing an onDeleted callback down to SearchRentalResultCard. Also ensure the mutation’s cache invalidation causes subsequent queries to stay consistent. Verify the component's usage instructions before implementing.
